turns.speaker: Speaker 1
turns.text: There was probably a five-year phase where we didn't do that very much.

turns.speaker: Speaker 1
turns.text: Uh, funnily enough, even when we first got married,

turns.speaker: Speaker 1
turns.text: we... And you're into this too, I don't know where she got this. Maybe it's infectious.

turns.speaker: Speaker 1
turns.text: So we would catch tadpoles as a married couple. We'd go down to the pond and scoop in and get some mucky-gucky tadpoles and watch them change into frogs. It's the miracle of life.

turns.speaker: Speaker 2
turns.text: You know what? I have a distinct memory on our honeymoon.

turns.speaker: Speaker 2
turns.text: We were taking a hike and you saw this. I think this was the first time I'd ever caught critters with you because I don't know, maybe when we were- She didn't- Maybe when we were dating, we just weren't in those situations. But I remember we were on a hike and you were like in a stream picking up rocks and you were so excited because this is a good salamander spot. There might be salamanders. And then you found one and I was so impressed.

turns.speaker: Speaker 1
turns.text: I didn't know what she was signing up for.
